data.title: The Ethereum Foundation announced the results of the ETH Rangers project: over $5.8 million in assets recovered/frozen.
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation announced that its co-sponsored ETH Rangers project has completed its six-month run. This project aimed to fund independent researchers who contribute to the public security of the Ethereum ecosystem. The 17 recipients achieved significant results in vulnerability research, security tool development, threat intelligence, and incident response, including: recovering or freezing over $5.8 million in funds, reporting or logging over 785 vulnerabilities and client-side issues, identifying approximately 100 attackers, providing security education content to over 209,000 users, and handling over 36 security incidents. Furthermore, the project encouraged over 800 teams to participate in security challenges, produced over 80 technical sharing sessions and training sessions, and developed or improved over 7 open-source security tools. The Ethereum Foundation stated that these achievements demonstrate the need for "decentralized defense" for decentralized networks and effectively enhance the overall security and resilience of the Ethereum ecosystem.

data.title: U.S. law firm Bernstein Liebhard has filed a class-action lawsuit against Gemini, alleging misrepresentation of information during its IPO.
data.description: US law firm Bernstein Liebhard announced it has filed a securities fraud class-action lawsuit on behalf of investors against Nasdaq-listed cryptocurrency exchange Gemini Space Station. The lawsuit alleges that Gemini made false statements about its business operations and prospects in its prospectus and related disclosures, particularly exaggerating the viability of its core crypto platform business. Investors wishing to serve as lead plaintiffs must submit their applications by May 18, 2026. Bernstein Liebhard is a well-known investor relations law firm that has previously filed class-action lawsuits against companies such as Ruhnn (the first publicly traded influencer e-commerce company), Apple, and AsiaInfo Technologies. (Globenewswire)

data.title: Both WTI and Brent crude oil prices extended their intraday losses, currently trading at $88.77 per barrel and $90.76 per barrel respectively.
data.description: According to Gate data, both WTI and Brent crude oil prices have fallen more than 4% today, currently trading at $88.77 per barrel and $90.76 per barrel respectively. In addition, the latest yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note is 4.27%, and the yield on the 2-year Treasury note is 3.74%.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi: Commercial vessel passage through the Strait of Hormuz is now open.
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi stated that commercial vessel passage through the Strait of Hormuz has been reopened. In accordance with the ceasefire agreement in Lebanon, the Iranian Ports and Maritime Organization has announced that, for the remainder of the ceasefire, all commercial vessels will have full access to the Strait of Hormuz, with routes entirely consistent with previously announced plans. (Jinshi)

data.title: French Finance Minister calls for the development of a stablecoin for the euro, potentially signaling a shift in French policy.
data.description: French Finance Minister Roland Lescure stated that Europe needs more euro-denominated stablecoins and encouraged EU banks to explore tokenized deposits. This statement is seen as a potential shift in the French government and central bank's stance. Lescure publicly supports the Qivalis consortium, comprised of 12 European banks including BBVA, ING, UniCredit, and BNP Paribas, which plans to launch a euro-pegged stablecoin in the second half of 2026 to counter the dollar's dominance in digital payments. He pointed out that the current market size of euro-denominated stablecoins is far smaller than that of dollar-denominated stablecoins, which is "unsatisfactory," and emphasized that promoting such development is "exactly what we need." In contrast, former Finance Minister Bruno Le Maire has strongly opposed privately issued stablecoins, arguing that they threaten monetary sovereignty; French central bank governor Francois Villeroy de Galhau recently warned of the risk of "monetary privatization" associated with stablecoins and tokenized currencies. Analysts believe that France's latest statement may indicate a more pragmatic approach to stablecoin and digital currency policies in Europe, attempting to strike a balance between regulation and innovation. (CoinDesk)

data.title: AI Investment Theme Gains Momentum Amid Market Recovery
data.description: Goldman Sachs has highlighted a resurgence in the AI investment theme as markets show signs of recovery. According to NS3.AI, Dominic Wilson noted that semiconductor stocks are performing strongly, reaching new highs. However, he cautioned that software stocks are experiencing more pressure. Wilson also warned that near-term volatility continues to pose a risk.

data.title: Soluna acquires Dorothy 1A project for $16.5 million to accelerate the expansion of its computing power park.
data.description: Soluna Holdings, a Nasdaq-listed company developing green data centers for computing-intensive applications such as Bitcoin mining and artificial intelligence, announced a $16.5 million acquisition of Spring Lane Capital's stake in Project Dorothy 1A (D1A), giving it 100% control of the project. This transaction marks another step in Soluna's vertical integration of the Dorothy campus, following its $53 million acquisition of the Briscoe wind farm earlier this month. The company stated it will continue to drive its transformation into a comprehensive computing hub centered on AI computing. (Businesswire)

data.title: ASTEROID Surges 44,923% Following Elon Musk's Viral Post
data.description: ASTEROID experienced a dramatic increase of 44,923% after U.S. President Donald Trump replied to a viral post associated with the Asteroid plush Shiba Inu. According to NS3.AI, Arkham identified a trader whose 1 ETH ASTEROID investment soared to nearly $474,000 within three hours. Additionally, ASTEROIDBOT saw a 15% rise due to the same narrative. The significant concentration of gains among a limited number of wallets suggests the potential for a sharp correction if early investors decide to take profits.

data.title: Compliance Systems Are Essential, Licenses Can Wait
data.description: According to PANews, the importance of establishing robust compliance systems is emphasized, while the acquisition of licenses can be deferred. This approach suggests prioritizing the development of a strong regulatory framework to ensure adherence to legal standards, while allowing flexibility in the timing of obtaining necessary licenses. The focus remains on maintaining compliance as a fundamental aspect of operations.

data.title: Bitcoin's Price Movement Amid Extreme Fear Index
data.description: Bitcoin is currently trading near $75,300 after briefly surpassing $76,300. According to NS3.AI, the Fear and Greed Index remains at 21, indicating extreme fear in the market. Analysts have observed that strong spot demand and ETF flows are contending with profit-taking, defensive derivatives strategies, and macroeconomic uncertainties.

data.title: The United States is considering a $20 billion cash-for-uranium deal with Iran.
data.description: According to AXIOS: The United States is considering a $20 billion cash-for-uranium deal with Iran. It is understood that the US may unfreeze $20 billion in funds in exchange for Iran relinquishing its enriched uranium stockpile. (Jinshi)

data.title: Both WTI and Brent crude oil prices fell by 3% intraday, currently trading at $89.70 per barrel and $91.15 per barrel respectively.
data.description: According to Gate data, both WTI and Brent crude oil fell by 3% intraday, currently trading at $89.70/barrel and $91.15/barrel respectively; the US dollar index DXY touched 98, down 0.22% intraday; spot silver touched $80/ounce, up 2.03% intraday.

data.title: Payward Expands in Regulated Markets with U.S. Crypto Derivatives Acquisition
data.description:
Payward has acquired control of a fully licensed U.S. crypto derivatives platform, marking a significant step in its expansion into regulated markets. According to CoinDesk, this acquisition allows Payward to enhance its offerings and strengthen its position in the competitive crypto industry. By gaining access to a licensed derivatives stack, Payward can now offer a broader range of services to its clients, aligning with its strategic goals of growth and compliance.

The acquisition is expected to provide Payward with a competitive edge as it navigates the complexities of the U.S. regulatory landscape. This move is part of Payward's broader strategy to establish a strong foothold in markets with stringent regulatory requirements. The company aims to leverage this acquisition to attract more institutional clients who prioritize compliance and security in their crypto dealings. As the crypto market continues to evolve, Payward's expansion into regulated derivatives is seen as a proactive measure to meet the increasing demand for compliant and secure trading options.

data.title: Payward plans to acquire Bitnomial for $550 million, accelerating its expansion into the US compliant derivatives market.
data.description: Payward, the parent company of crypto exchange Kraken, announced it will acquire 100% of digital asset derivatives platform Bitnomial for up to $550 million (cash + stock). The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2026, subject to regulatory approval. Bitnomial is the first crypto-native platform to simultaneously hold all three licenses for the full stack of derivatives in the United States (designated contract market, derivatives clearing, and futures commission broker). This acquisition will help Payward quickly acquire a complete compliant derivatives infrastructure, significantly accelerating its expansion in the US market. Upon completion of the transaction, Bitnomial's clearing and trading capabilities will be integrated with platforms such as Kraken and NinjaTrader, offering US clients products such as spot margin trading, perpetual contracts, and options, and will be regulated by the CFTC. This acquisition will also expand Payward Services, enabling banks, fintech companies, and brokerages to access the compliant US derivatives market through a single API. Industry insiders believe that with market recovery and valuation repair, M&A activity in the crypto sector is picking up again. Leading institutions are acquiring companies to enhance their key capabilities in compliance, custody, and derivatives, accelerating their transformation into institutional-grade comprehensive trading platforms. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Payward to Acquire Bitnomial in $550 Million Deal
data.description: Payward has announced an agreement to acquire Bitnomial for up to $550 million in a combination of cash and stock. According to NS3.AI, Bitnomial possesses the necessary approvals to function as a designated contract market, a derivatives clearing organization, and a futures commission merchant within the United States. The acquisition is anticipated to be finalized in the first half of 2026, subject to customary conditions and regulatory approvals.

data.title: Bank of England Economist Highlights Inflation Target Risks
data.description: The Bank of England's Chief Economist, Huw Pill, has expressed concerns about the risks associated with achieving the inflation target. According to Jin10, Pill emphasized the need to consider policies that would effectively prevent a recurrence of the economic challenges faced in 2022. He highlighted the importance of strategic measures to ensure stability and avoid repeating past mistakes in managing inflation.

data.title: OKX Wallet passed SlowMist security audit; all 7 core modules passed.
data.description: According to its official website, OKX Wallet has completed a security audit by the blockchain security firm SlowMist. The results show that all seven core security modules passed, with no high-risk security issues found. The audit covered key aspects such as private key management and cryptographic security mechanisms, demonstrating that all sensitive information is processed locally, and no risk of private keys or mnemonic phrases being leaked was detected. The full audit report is now publicly available on the website.

data.title: Bitget IPO Prime's first project, preSPAX, will open for spot trading on April 21st at 20:00.
data.description: According to the official announcement, in order to optimize the trading experience and liquidity, the listing market for Bitget IPO Prime's first project, preSPAX, will be changed from "over-the-counter trading" to "spot trading." Trading will open on April 21st at 20:00 (UTC+8), and the instant swap function will be available 10 minutes after trading begins.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | China and India Drive Global Gold Demand Over the Past Decade
data.description: China and India have significantly influenced global gold demand, according to data from The Kobeissi Letter. Over the past decade, these two countries have collectively accounted for nearly half of the world's gold purchases, with China contributing 27% and India 21%. According to NS3.AI, emerging economies have played a major role, representing approximately 70% of global gold acquisitions during this period.

data.title: Cyberspace Administration of China: Strengthen legislation in areas such as artificial intelligence, digital economy, and blockchain.
data.description: The official WeChat account of the Cyberspace Administration of China published an article entitled "Building a Law-Based Cyberspace that Benefits the People," which pointed out that it is necessary to continuously improve laws and regulations that promote and protect the development of informatization, especially to strengthen legislation in areas such as artificial intelligence, digital economy, network infrastructure, satellite internet, and blockchain, and strive to ensure that cyber legislation covers wherever informatization development goes, so as to safeguard technological innovation and self-reliance in the cyberspace field with the rule of law.
